title: AD Taylor-tower P7: tensor-valued Taylor towers -- high-order AD through the ML operator path
goal: Implement docs/design/AD_TAYLOR_TOWER.md sec 10 (enhancement 4): generalize the Taylor tower from scalar coefficients to tensor coefficients so order>=3 derivatives work through ML-style tensor computations (elementwise chains, matmul, conv2d, activation compositions) parameterized by a scalar (scale/temperature/time) variable. Order<=2 tensor AD (the existing reverse-mode/jet path) must stay byte-identical -- the tower only engages for an explicit order>=3 request, mirroring the scalar dispatch discipline of P1-P6.
acceptance: f(t) = sum(A .* t^3) (elementwise): d^3f/dt^3 = 6*sum(A) exactly, plus d0/d1/d2/d4 vs the closed-form cubic, at multiple t0 including t0=0, plus a truncation-order self-consistency check (K=1 vs K=4 agree on shared coefficients)
matmul chain f(t) = sum((t*A) matmul (t*B)): derivatives to order 4 vs the closed form h(t)=sum(matmul(A,B))*t^2 (h'''=h''''=0), across square and non-square shapes and multiple t0
conv2d chain (bonus bilinear-inner-op coverage): f(t) = sum(conv2d(image, t*K, 1)), linear in t, vs closed form (order>=2 vanish)

activation composition: sigmoid AND tanh chains f(t) = sum(sigmoid/tanh(C .* t)) to order 3 vs FINITE-DIFFERENCE-FREE analytic closed forms (the sigmoid/tanh autonomous-ODE Bell-polynomial derivative formulas), for multiple (C, t0)
order<=2 tensor AD byte-identical: zero existing files modified (only new files + a CMakeLists add_test), so the existing jet/reverse-tape tensor path is untouched by construction

regressions hold: tests/ad/taylor_tower_test.esk 52/52, tests/ad/taylor_tower_mono_test.esk 441/441, tests/ad/guw_multivariate_test.esk 35/35, tests/ad/reverse_over_taylor_test.esk 18/18, scripts/run_ad_depth.sh gate=PASS regressions=0, scripts/run_ad_oracle.sh 56/56, scripts/run_sicp_smoke.sh 88/88, scripts/run_ml_tests.sh 42/42, tests/v1_3_edge_cases/ad_input2_test.esk 6/6 (ad_input2_* smoke probes)
new test tests/ad/tensor_tower_test.esk PASS under BOTH -r (JIT, ctest tensor_tower_runtime_smoke) and AOT (manual -o build + execute, exit 0)
campaign_payoff: High-order AD now reaches the ML operator path (elementwise/matmul/conv2d/activation chains parameterized by a scalar), the specific gap the AD smoke probes (ad_input2_*) exercise only at order<=1 gradients today. Closes the tensor half of the Taylor-tower design (sec 10) with zero risk to the order<=2 hot path or any existing tensor kernel, using the same 'pure orchestration over the existing kernel, no codegen churn' strategy that made P4 (GUW) safe and fast to land.

delivered.representation_decision: TOWER OF TENSORS (a Scheme vector of K+1 tensors sharing one shape), NOT tensor-of-towers (each tensor element holding a scalar tower). This is the only representation compatible with the existing dense tensor runtime without reworking it: HEAP_SUBTYPE_TENSOR stores homogeneous raw doubles in a flat backing array (design sec 4/10) -- a single element slot cannot hold a tagged heap-pointer AD value without boxing every element, which would turn every tensor kernel (shape ops, broadcast, BLAS, Metal/CUDA) into a generic per-element dispatch loop. Tower-of-tensors sidesteps this: each COEFFICIENT is an ordinary, completely unmodified HEAP_SUBTYPE_TENSOR value, so every existing tensor primitive (tensor-add/sub/neg/scale/mul, matmul, conv2d, tensor-exp, tensor-div, tensor-sum) is reused UNCHANGED as the inner op of a Cauchy convolution over the series index -- exactly the sec-10 claim that 'the convolution over series index k is orthogonal to the tensor contraction over spatial/feature axes'. Implemented as a pure library-level module (lib/core/ad/tensor_tower.esk), not a new C heap-subtype/LLVM-codegen tier -- mirroring how P4 (GUW, lib/core/ad/guw.esk) built multivariate high-order AD as pure orchestration with zero codegen changes and zero risk to order<=2.
delivered.why_plain_tensor_ops_dont_already_propag: Confirmed empirically (not assumed): unlike scalar +/-/*/exp (polymorphic runtime dispatchers that already understand a Taylor-tower operand -- the mechanism GUW rides for free), the tensor builtins (tensor-scale, matmul, ...) convert a scalar argument straight to a raw double at the call boundary, silently collapsing a live tower to its primal value. `(derivative-n (lambda (t) (tensor-sum (tensor-scale A (* t t t)))) t0 3)` returns 0, not 6*sum(A), because the tower never survives tensor-scale. A second, unrelated pre-existing gap was also found and left untouched (out of P7 scope, order<=1 not order>=3): `(gradient (lambda (t) (tensor-sum (tensor-scale A t))) t0)` also returns 0 -- reverse-mode AD's 'input2' plumbing (docs sec 10 reference) covers a TENSOR operand of conv2d/batch-norm/layer-norm/attention (verified working in tests/v1_3_edge_cases/ad_input2_test.esk) but not a plain scalar multiplier threaded through tensor-scale. This is why the P7 API requires the tensor computation to be expressed via the new tt-* combinators rather than 'just working' on an arbitrary existing tensor-op composition -- documented as the scope boundary, not silently worked around.
delivered.module: lib/core/ad/tensor_tower.esk (new core AD module, require core.ad.tensor_tower). Self-contained, no stdlib map/for-each dependency (core module discipline, following lib/core/ad/{tape,guw}.esk). Every tensor builtin (tensor-add/sub/neg/scale/mul, matmul, conv2d, tensor-exp/div/sum) is a compiler intrinsic recognised only in direct call position -- confirmed empirically that passing one as a first-class value into a generic higher-order helper fails with 'Undefined variable' at the call site, and 'variable is not a procedure' at the call. Every combinator therefore calls its tensor op by name directly (no `tt-map`/generic-`inner-op` abstraction), at the cost of duplicating the add/sub/neg and the three Cauchy-convolution variants (Hadamard/matmul/conv2d). All loops use pure accumulator-passing named-let recursion (no scalar `set!`), only vector-set! on a freshly allocated write-once-per-index vector -- avoiding the historical named-let/closure mutable-capture bug class entirely by construction.
delivered.api: tt-const T K; tt-from-scalar-series T coeffs (coeffs = an existing scalar `taylor` coefficient list -- this is the literal 'wiring' of derivative-n/taylor: the pure-scalar sub-series of t is computed by the UNMODIFIED existing scalar tower kernel, then lifted to tensors); tt-add/tt-sub/tt-neg/tt-scale-const (elementwise linear); tt-hadamard-cauchy/tt-matmul-cauchy/tt-conv2d-cauchy (Cauchy convolution with a concrete bilinear inner op, sec 10 'mul -> conv where appropriate'); tt-exp/tt-div (elementwise nonlinear lift, mirrors runtime_taylor.c tr_exp/tr_div verbatim with scalar-multiply -> Hadamard tensor-mul); tt-sigmoid/tt-tanh (composed from tt-exp/tt-div exactly as runtime_taylor.c's own tanh is built from dual exp); tt-sum (per-coefficient tensor-sum -> a plain scalar Taylor coefficient list); tt-nth-derivative coeffs n = n!*coeffs[n] (mirrors eshkol_taylor_extract); tt-value/tt-order.
delivered.ops_covered: elementwise linear (add/sub/negate/scale-by-scalar-series) any order K, exact; Hadamard elementwise multiply and elementwise exp/div lift (so any composition of +,-,*,exp,div,sigmoid,tanh built from tt-* combinators works to any order K); matmul via Cauchy convolution (bilinear tensor contraction as the inner op); conv2d via Cauchy convolution (linear-in-kernel case verified; the general bilinear image x kernel case uses the identical mechanism).
delivered.ops_deferred: batch-norm/layer-norm/scaled-dot-attention as ONE lifted tower op: each decomposes into axis-aware reductions (per-channel mean/variance/rsqrt) that have no tower analogue yet -- an axis-aware tt-mean/tt-rsqrt pair is the natural follow-up once needed; they remain available at order<=1 via the existing untouched reverse-mode path (ad_input2_test.esk). GPU dispatch of the per-coefficient op is unaffected either way -- the series loop is host Scheme exactly as sec 10 specifies ('the series loop stays on host, the per-index tensor op dispatches to device'); each inner tensor-mul/matmul/conv2d call already dispatches to BLAS/Metal/CUDA precisely as it does outside a tower.
delivered.tests: tests/ad/tensor_tower_test.esk: 56 checks -- elementwise A.*t^3 (value + d1..d4 + truncation-order self-consistency) at 3 t0 including t0=0; matmul chain to order 4 (value + d1..d4, d3/d4 exact zero) at 3 (shape, t0) combos including a non-square 2x3 . 3x2 case; conv2d chain (value + d1, d2/d3 exact zero) at 2 (kernel, t0) combos; sigmoid and tanh activation chains to order 3 vs closed-form analytic derivatives (no finite differences) at 2 (C, t0) combos each. Registered in CMakeLists.txt as tensor_tower_runtime_smoke (JIT -r via ctest, PASS/FAIL regex) and verified separately under AOT (-o build, execute, exit 0) -- both 56/56.

remainder: batch-norm/layer-norm/scaled-dot-attention lifted to the tower (order>=3) is explicit future work pending an axis-aware tt-mean/tt-rsqrt pair; order<=1 through these ops is unaffected and already covered by ad_input2_test.esk.

The order<=1 tensor-AD gap discovered incidentally (reverse-mode gradient through tensor-scale's plain scalar multiplier returns 0, unlike its TENSOR-operand 'input2' siblings conv2d/batch-norm/layer-norm/attention) is out of P7 scope (order<=1, not order>=3) and left untouched; worth a dedicated follow-up ticket.

The library-level 'tower of tensors' is exposed as explicit new combinators (tt-*), not as a silent extension of ordinary tensor-op composition under derivative-n/taylor -- an ordinary tensor-op-composed function does not automatically gain order>=3 support (the tower would need every relevant tensor builtin's runtime call boundary changed to recognise a Taylor-tower-tagged scalar argument, i.e. the New-numeric-type checklist of design sec 12 applied to every tensor op's scalar argument -- a materially larger, higher-risk codegen change than this phase's pragmatic-first-cut scope calls for, and explicitly not attempted here to keep order<=2 risk at zero).

verifications.evidence: tests/ad/tensor_tower_test.esk 56/56 PASS under BOTH -r (JIT, ctest tensor_tower_runtime_smoke, 2.0s) and AOT (exit 0). Elementwise A.*t^3: d3=6*sum(A) exact at t0 in {1.7,-0.3,0.0}, d4=0, K=1 vs K=4 truncation-invariant. Matmul chain to order 4: d0/d1/d2 match t^2*sum(matmul(A,B)) closed form, d3=d4=0 exactly, verified square (2x2 x 2x2) and non-square (2x3 x 3x2) shapes at 3 t0 values. Conv2d chain: d0/d1 match closed form, d2/d3=0 exactly (conv2d linear-in-kernel case), 2 kernel/t0 combos. Sigmoid/tanh activation chains to order 3: matched against independently-derived closed-form Bell-polynomial derivatives of the sigmoid/tanh autonomous ODE (no finite differences), max abs err < 1e-9, 2 (C,t0) combos each x2 activations x4 orders = 16 checks. Regressions: taylor_tower_test 52/52, taylor_tower_mono_test 441/441, guw_multivariate_test 35/35, reverse_over_taylor_test 18/18 (JET8 parity witness still PASS), scripts/run_ad_depth.sh gate=PASS regressions=0 improvements=12 (unchanged from P4/P5 baseline), scripts/run_ad_oracle.sh 56/56, scripts/run_sicp_smoke.sh 88/88, scripts/run_ml_tests.sh 42/42, tests/v1_3_edge_cases/ad_input2_test.esk 6/6. Zero existing files modified other than the CMakeLists.txt add_test registration -- order<=2 tensor AD byte-identical by construction (no shared code touched).
